short: A memory trick that stores the KV cache in small reusable blocks like OS paging.
definition: PagedAttention is the KV cache memory-management algorithm introduced by vLLM (SOSP 2023). Instead of pre-allocating one large contiguous block of GPU memory per request, it splits the cache into fixed-size blocks (16 tokens by default) mapped through a block table to non-contiguous physical memory. This is virtual memory for GPUs: it nearly eliminates fragmentation and over-allocation, letting many more requests share the GPU.
